input.premise: A burned-out forensic accountant discovers her firm has been laundering money for a criminal syndicate — and that her mentor set her up to take the fall.
input.protagonist: Nadia Voss. Want: expose the syndicate and clear her name. Need: stop letting loyalty to authority figures override her own judgment.
input.tone: Grounded thriller. References: Michael Clayton, The Insider.

test_cases.notes: All 15 beats present, correctly placed, each a concrete scene. Internal arc legible at Midpoint, All Is Lost, Final Image. Structural assessment identifies the specific weak beat with actionable reasoning.

input.premise: A grief counselor who secretly hasn't processed her own husband's death begins treating a patient who describes the same accident in which her husband died — from the other driver's perspective.
input.protagonist: Dr. Sera Malik. Want: understand how the accident really happened. Need: allow herself to grieve rather than manage grief in others.
input.antagonist: Her own avoidance and the patient's unreliable memory
input.tone: Psychological drama, quiet and unsettling. References: In Treatment, The Affair.

test_cases.notes: Beat placement correctly adapted to 60-minute pilot structure. Internal antagonism handled well. Consistency docked one point: B Story introduced a minor character not present in premise.

input.premise: Two estranged brothers reunite to settle their late father's fishing lodge and discover he had a second family living forty miles away.
input.protagonist: Older brother Cal. Want: sell the lodge fast and return to his life. Need: accept that his father was a complicated man — and so is he.
input.antagonist: The revelation itself, and the half-sibling who stands to inherit equally
input.tone: Quiet drama with dark comedy undertones. References: Nebraska, Ordinary People.

test_cases.notes: Excellent handling of ensemble dynamics. Fun and Games section particularly strong. Coherence docked one point: All Is Lost beat felt underdifferentiated from Break into Three in this lower-stakes premise.
